api: SavvyCal API v1 (https://api.savvycal.com; documented at https://developers.savvycal.com/, fetched 2026-07-03)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against the complete documented SavvyCal REST API (developers.savvycal.com's Events/Scheduling Links/Current User/Time Zones/Webhooks/Workflows categories). 7 read streams: the 3 legacy-parity streams (events, links, contacts — contacts is legacy-only, see docs.md) plus time_zones, webhooks, workflows, and workflow_rules (fan_out over workflows). 9 write actions: link create (personal-scope), link create (team/individual scope), link update, link delete, link duplicate, link toggle (active/disabled), event cancel, webhook create, webhook delete. Excluded: event creation (a booking-flow-shaped multi-party payload with no plain flat record schema suited to a reverse-ETL write), the link-slots availability lookup (requires an undocumented real start/end date-range query window a synthetic fixture cannot honestly exercise), single-event/single-link/single-webhook/single-time-zone detail GETs that duplicate an already-covered list stream's record shape, and the current-user introspection endpoint.

excluded.reason: single-event detail; identical record shape to the events list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

creates an event by booking a specific scheduling link's availability (attendee + time-slot-selection booking flow), not a plain CRM-style record create; no documented flat request schema suited to a reverse-ETL write action
single-link detail; identical record shape to the links list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

requires a real start/end availability-window query (undocumented exact parameter names on the server-rendered schema panel this pass could not scrape); a fan_out stream built on a guessed query shape risks silently returning empty/wrong results rather than an honest, verified read
authenticated-user profile introspection, not a syncable object stream
single-time-zone detail; identical record shape to the time_zones list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-webhook detail; identical record shape to the webhooks list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
